search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk ng126 pregnancy bleeding pain assessment ultrasound hCG safety referral 2026
site:dha.gov.ae early pregnancy bleeding emergency guideline
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic ectopic-pregnancy, miscarriage or pregnancy-of-unknown-location conclusion
automatic ultrasound, hCG, treatment, surgery or referral
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Symptoms and initial results do not establish the diagnosis.
No investigation, procedure, treatment, medicine, dose or referral is generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: People with bleeding in early pregnancy; later gestation, postpartum bleeding, trauma and non-pregnancy bleeding require different pathways.
UAE_applicability: Clinical domains transfer, but UAE obstetric, gynecology, ultrasound, laboratory, Rh-status, consent, referral and emergency pathways govern.
recency_verification: Current NICE NG126 recommendations (2026 registered version) were reviewed on 2026-07-14.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact NICE early-pregnancy section supports documenting onset, severity, associated symptoms, relevant negatives, concerns, clinician risk context, measured vital data, focused assessment, appearance and time-critical symptoms without diagnosing ectopic pregnancy or miscarriage.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists pain, amenorrhoea, bleeding, gastrointestinal symptoms, dizziness or syncope, shoulder-tip pain, urinary symptoms, tenderness, haemodynamic signs, pregnancy testing, and escalation criteria.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact ultrasound section supports recording existing tests and imaging already performed or reviewed, without recommending or interpreting them.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines clinician-performed transvaginal or selected transabdominal ultrasound and cautions against overinterpreting one early scan or LMP alone.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact hCG and safety section supports documenting clinician-decided escalation, specialist discussion and arranged handover or follow-up, without setting thresholds or intervals.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Requires symptoms to take priority over hCG, limits hCG interpretation to appropriate clinical use, and requires clear worsening-symptom access and follow-up.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact service section supports a clinician-entered plan and completed risk or consent discussion only when documented; it does not create management.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines the up-to-13-week complication population and services offering specialist assessment, ultrasound, serum hCG review, and timely referral.

unresolved_source_gaps: Gestational age, last menstrual period, bleeding amount, tissue, pain detail, syncope, obstetric history, Rh status, pelvic examination, test values, diagnosis, treatment and disposition remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
